scheme_name: A Scheme to Provide Assistance for Building E-Market Facilities in Market Committees
page_title: A Scheme to Provide Assistance for Building E-Market Facilities in Market Committees

details: "A Scheme to Provide Assistance for Building E-Market Facilities in Market Committees" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to support market committees to set up e-market facilities, enhancing transparency and efficiency in agricultural marketing across the state.
benefits: ₹1,00,000/- (One Lakh Rupees) per eligible market committee.
eligibility: Co-operative Market Committees shall be eligible under this scheme.
The committee must operate within Gujarat.

offline: Fill in the application form on plain paper as per the instructions.
Submit the completed application to the District Registrar for processing.

documents_required: Proof of cooperative market committee registration.
Details of the proposed e-market facility setup.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the "A Scheme to Provide Assistance for Building E-Market Facilities in Market Committees"?
faqs.answer: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial support to cooperative market committees in Gujarat for establishing digital e-market (e-NAM) facilities under the National Agriculture Market Policy.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Only registered cooperative market committees operating within the state of Gujarat are eligible to apply for this scheme.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Under the scheme, each eligible market committee is granted a financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- (One Lakh Rupees).

faqs.question: Is there any fee for obtaining or submitting the application form?
faqs.answer: No, there is no fee required for obtaining or submitting the application form; it is completely free of cost.

faqs.question: Can private companies or individuals apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only cooperative market committees are eligible to apply; private entities or individuals are not eligible.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable outside Gujarat?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is limited to cooperative market committees located within the state of Gujarat.

faqs.question: Is online application available for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, currently, there is no provision for submitting applications online; applications must be submitted physically to the District Registrar.

faqs.question: Which department is responsible for implementing the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation, Government of Gujarat, is responsible for implementing the scheme.
